topNews.title: Show HN: Kiyeovo 1.0 release- dual network-mode decentralized E2EE P2P messenger
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Kiyeovo is a decentralized, end-to-end encrypted messenger. It requires no accounts, no email, no central servers. Peers find each other and talk directly over libp2p. kad-DHT stores messages for offline peers and handles finding other users by their username/peerID. After almost a year of solo…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Kiyeovo 1.0, a decentralized end-to-end encrypted P2P messenger, was released.  
• Why it matters: It eliminates the need for accounts and central servers, enhancing user privacy and security.  
• Business opportunity: There is potential for growth in the secure communication market, appealing to privacy-conscious users.

topNews.title: Show HN: Arcaide – Explore code with multi-level call graphs
topNews.rawTextSnippet: One of the things I do when approaching a new codebase is to find the entry points and start exploring down the call paths. topNews.summary:
• What happened: Arcaide launched a tool for visualizing multi-level call graphs in codebases.  
• Why it matters: This tool enhances understanding of code structure and relationships, improving developer efficiency.  
• Business opportunity: There is potential for software development firms to integrate Arcaide into their workflows for better onboarding and code management.

dailyBrief: Today's AI landscape is marked by significant developments, including Anthropic's classifiers for Fable, which have been criticized for being overly cautious, potentially impacting user experience and innovation. Meanwhile, the launch of FableCut, a browser-based video editor powered by AI, promises to streamline content creation without dependencies, appealing to businesses seeking efficiency. In geopolitical news, China's interest in Nvidia chips highlights the ongoing tech rivalry and its implications for global supply chains. Additionally, Nandan Nilekani's departure from his GP role at Fundamentum coincides with the firm's launch of a $200 million third fund, signaling continued investment in AI-driven startups. Lastly, the introduction of Muse Spark 1.1 showcases advancements in creative AI tools, further enhancing productivity for professionals across various sectors.
